commit.message:
refactor: centralize delegate migration into legacy_delegates.toml (#147)

* refactor: centralize delegate migration into legacy_delegates.toml

Replace the fragile, multi-location delegate migration system with a
single source of truth and automated tooling.

Before:
- Two separate LEGACY_DELEGATES lists (delegate lib.rs + UI chat_delegate.rs)
  in incompatible formats (hex strings vs byte arrays)
- Manual key computation with external tool
- CI used fragile grep pattern that required "code_hash" in comments
- No validation that entries were correct
- 3 WASM locations to manually sync

After:
- legacy_delegates.toml: single source of truth with human-readable hex
- ui/build.rs: generates Rust const array at compile time from TOML
- scripts/add-migration.sh: one command to compute keys and add entry
- scripts/sync-wasm.sh: one command to build and copy all WASMs
- scripts/check-migration.sh: validates migration entry exists
- common/tests/migration_test.rs: validates BLAKE3 derivation
- CI checks legacy_delegates.toml directly (no grep hack)
- cargo make tasks: add-migration, sync-wasm, check-migration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove cargo:rerun-if-changed to preserve build timestamp freshness

Codex review found that adding cargo:rerun-if-changed disables Cargo's
default "always re-run" behavior for the entire build script, making
BUILD_TIMESTAMP_ISO and GIT_COMMIT_HASH stale during incremental builds.

Instead, always regenerate the legacy_delegates.rs file but only write
it when content actually changed, avoiding unnecessary recompilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use proper TOML parser in migration tests and add hex length validation

Address review findings:
- Replace fragile hand-rolled line parser in migration_test.rs with
  toml + serde deserialization (matches build.rs approach)
- Add no_duplicate_delegate_keys test alongside existing code_hash check
- Add explicit hex length validation (== 64 chars) in build.rs
- Add odd-length hex string guard in migration_test.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add migration_test to build workflow

The new migration_test validates legacy_delegates.toml entries but was
not wired into CI. Add it alongside the existing delegate_key_test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: AppB削除後の累計データをGAS再集計で自動反映

【問題】
localStorage の ruikei_v7_{date}_{uchi} はAppAが送信するたびに
加算保存されるが、AppBでサーバー側の記録を削除してもAppAの
キャッシュには一切反映されなかった。

【解決策】
手動リセットボタン方式をやめ、日付・打ち手を変更した際に
GAS（?action=records）からレコードを取得して累計キャッシュを
完全再構築することで自動連動させる。

【変更内容】
- triggerRuikeiSync(): 400msデバウンス付き同期起動関数を追加
- syncRuikeiFromGAS(): 非同期でGASからレコード取得し、
  同日・同打ち手のレコードを集計して ruikei_v7_* を上書き、
  最後に recalc() を呼んでリアルタイム表示を更新
- buildUchiChips(): 打ち手チップクリック時に triggerRuikeiSync() を追加
- #in-date: 日付変更時に recalc() + triggerRuikeiSync() を追加
- 前回追加した「AppB削除後リセット」ボタンと resetRuikeiData() を削除

【動作フロー】
AppB削除 → AppAで打ち手 or 日付を切り替え → GAS再集計 → 正しい値に更新

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix chest item counts always showing x1 on Minecraft 1.21+

so all items defaulted to count 1.

commit.message:
Add Mermaid diagram generation to PR review comments

New diagram agent runs in parallel with other agents (light model) and
produces a Mermaid diagram (flowchart/sequence/class) visualising the
structure of changes. Rendered as a collapsible section in the PR comment
using GitHub's native Mermaid support. Stored on the review record and
shown in the dashboard drawer. Controlled by the existing Diagram toggle
in Settings (on by default). Skips trivial diffs automatically.

commit.message:
Exclude minecraft-autoshutdown.service from _list_instances()

The 'minecraft-*.service' glob matched minecraft-autoshutdown.service,
causing it to appear in both the admin wrapper's server list and the
autoshutdown script's idle check. In the autoshutdown case this was a
latent correctness bug: under timer execution the service is active
and has no WorkingDirectory, so it would always produce a NOT IDLE
verdict and block shutdown. Fixed in both files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add connection diagnostics and JoyToKey solution guide

Critical findings from testing:
- Wired connection works partially (sends plain arrows, not Win+Arrow)
- Wireless dongle NOT detected by 3DxWare
- BUT JoyToKey DOES detect wireless SpaceMouse!
- Notepad test confirms 3DxWare sends keyboard events (Up/Down arrows)
- Desktop.xml being ignored - only plain arrows sent, no Windows modifier

Created connection-test.md:
- Step-by-step diagnostic tests (Notepad, on-screen keyboard, keycode.info)
- Identifies exact point of failure in the chain
- Determines if 3DxWare is sending keys or not

Created wireless-vs-wired-issue.md:
- Compares wireless vs wired connection behavior
- Documents that JoyToKey detects wireless but 3DxWare doesn't
- Complete JoyToKey configuration guide as alternative solution
- Axis-to-keyboard mapping instructions
- Deadzone and sensitivity settings
- Why 3DxWare XML approach failed

Key discovery: JoyToKey can read SpaceMouse wireless input directly
This bypasses 3DxWare entirely and may be simpler/more reliable solution.

Recommended next step:
Configure JoyToKey to map SpaceMouse axes to Win+Arrow shortcuts
This should work for both wireless and wired connections.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
chore: upgrade Fresh 1.6.3 → 2.2.0 (#1175)

* chore: upgrade Fresh 1.6.3 → 2.2.0

Migrate from Fresh 1.x (deno.land/x) to Fresh 2.2.0 (jsr:@fresh/core).

Key changes:
- Replace $fresh/ import map with jsr:@fresh/core and explicit subpath entries
  (fresh/dev, fresh/runtime) to avoid esbuild trailing-slash resolution issues
- Rewrite main.ts to use App + staticFiles() + fsRoutes() pattern;
  remove start(manifest, { render }) and fresh.gen.ts manifest
- Rewrite dev.ts to use Builder.listen(() => import('./main.ts')) factory pattern
- Migrate all handlers from Handlers<Data>/ctx.render() to page() helper
  with pageCacheHeaders(); ctx.renderNotFound() → throw new HttpError(404)
- Update middleware and handler signatures from (req, ctx) to (ctx: FreshContext)
- Implement Stitches CSS SSR via middleware reset() + sync _app.tsx getCssText()
  leveraging Fresh 2.x two-pass rendering (route renders first, then _app.tsx)
- Add getNormalizeCss() sync accessor to avoid async _app.tsx breaking CSS order
- Add explicit 404 handler to _404.tsx to return HTTP 404 status
- Update Head imports from $fresh/runtime.ts to fresh/runtime
- Upgrade @preact/signals to npm:@preact/signals@^2.5.0
- Replace preact/ trailing-slash entry with explicit npm subpath entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove invalid 'root' option from App constructor

FreshConfig does not include a 'root' property; it is set internally
by the framework. Passing it caused a TS2353 type error in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove console

* chore: remove debug log, parallelize fetches, unify cache-control logic

- domains/privacy/pages/PrivacyPolicy.tsx: remove debug console.log(route)
- domains/home/pages/Home.handler.ts: run getRepositories/getHomeWidgets/getPosts
  in parallel with Promise.all (was sequential ~3x slower)
- shared/middleware/cache.ts: extract buildCacheControlValue() to eliminate
  duplicated cache-control header string logic between cacheMiddleware and
  pageCacheHeaders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace FreshContext with Context, fix createActionComponent types

- Replace deprecated FreshContext with Context<unknown> across all handlers
  and middleware (FreshContext = Context<unknown> per Fresh 2.x docs)
- core/ui/create_action_component.ts: make generic over ElementType so
  JSX.IntrinsicElements[T] provides correct element-specific attributes
  (fixes pre-existing TS2353 error where rel was not in Attributes type)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add lang="ja" to html element

Render explicit <html lang="ja"><head><body> structure in _app.tsx so
Fresh 2.x uses it instead of the default wrapper (renderedHtmlTag=true).
Moves script tags into <head> and page content into <body>.

* chore: update deno.lock

commit.message:
Add composite access gap score identifying potentially underserved neighborhoods (#19) (#47)

Implements a 0-100 composite score combining three independent signals:
- Low 311 engagement rate (35% weight)
- Low transit accessibility (30% weight)
- High non-English speaking percentage (35% weight)

Each signal is min-max normalized across all SD communities. Communities
need at least 2 signals to receive a score. Results are cached for 24h.

New files:
- server/services/gap-analysis.ts: Core scoring engine
- server/routes/gap-analysis.ts: /api/access-gap endpoint + /ranking

UI uses appropriately cautious language ("may face," "patterns suggest")
per issue requirements. Score displayed in amber-themed sidebar section
with individual signal breakdowns and community ranking.

commit.message:
Fix vector search: optimize zvec collections after upserts (#225)

* Fix vector search by optimizing zvec collections after upserts

zvec requires optimize() to compact WAL data into indexed segments.
Without it, queries fail with "vector indexer not found for field
embedding" because unindexed WAL segments have no vector indexer.

commit.message:
fix: implement Standard Webhooks signature verification for Polar

Previous implementation used simple HMAC-SHA256 on body only.
Polar uses Standard Webhooks spec (svix):
- Headers: svix-id, svix-timestamp, svix-signature
- Signed content: msg_id.timestamp.body
- Secret: base64 encoded with whsec_ prefix
- Signature: v1,{base64_hmac}

commit.message:
fix: unify mNAV calculation — single source of truth via mnav-engine (#330)

Two separate mNAV functions existed:
- mnav-calculation.ts (used by sidebar stats via use-mnav-stats.ts)
- mnav-engine.ts (used by data table and company pages)

They differed in warrant proceeds handling — the engine version added
warrant proceeds to both cash and restrictedCash (cancelling out),
while the calculation version correctly added only to cash.

Changes:
- Fix mnav-engine.ts: warrant proceeds add to cash only (not restrictedCash)
- Point use-mnav-stats.ts to mnav-engine.ts instead of mnav-calculation.ts
- All mNAV computations now flow through a single function

This resolves B-13/B-22 from QA report: sidebar showed different
mNAV stats than company pages and sector statistics.
